subtopics.name: Newton's First Law (Law of Inertia)
subtopics.content: A body remains at rest or in uniform motion in a straight line unless acted upon by an external force. Inertia is the tendency of a body to resist changes in its state of motion. Mass is a measure of inertia.
competences: Explain Newton's First Law with real-world examples

subtopics.name: Newton's Second Law (F = ma)
subtopics.content: The rate of change of momentum of a body is directly proportional to the applied force and takes place in the direction of the force. F = ma, where F is the net force in Newtons, m is mass in kg, and a is acceleration in m/s². When mass is constant: F = ma. When mass varies (e.g., rockets): F = dp/dt. Impulse = F×t = change in momentum.
competences: State and derive F = ma from momentum principles
Solve problems involving force, mass, and acceleration

subtopics.name: Newton's Third Law (Action-Reaction)
subtopics.content: For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. The action and reaction forces act on different bodies. They are equal in magnitude, opposite in direction, and of the same type. Examples: book on table (weight and normal reaction), walking (foot pushes ground, ground pushes foot), rocket propulsion (exhaust gases pushed down, rocket pushed up).
competences: Explain why action-reaction pairs do not cancel out
Apply to walking, swimming, rocket propulsion

subtopics.name: Equations of Motion (SUVAT)
subtopics.content: For uniformly accelerated motion: v = u + at, s = ut + ½at², v² = u² + 2as, s = ½(u+v)t. Where u = initial velocity, v = final velocity, a = acceleration, s = displacement, t = time. These apply only when acceleration is constant. For free fall: a = g = 9.81 m/s² downward.
competences: Apply SUVAT equations to solve motion problems
Calculate displacement from area under v-t graph

subtopics.name: Projectile Motion
subtopics.content: Projectile motion is motion under gravity alone. Horizontal component of velocity is constant (no air resistance). Vertical component is affected by gravity (g = 9.81 m/s²). Time of flight: T = 2u sin(θ)/g. Range: R = u² sin(2θ)/g. Maximum height: H = u² sin²(θ)/(2g). Maximum range occurs at θ = 45°.
competences: Resolve velocity into horizontal and vertical components
Calculate time of flight, range, maximum height

subtopics.name: Uniform Circular Motion
subtopics.content: An object moving in a circle at constant speed has centripetal acceleration directed toward the centre: a = v²/r = ω²r. Centripetal force: F = mv²/r = mω²r. Angular velocity: ω = 2πf = 2π/T. Period T = 2π/ω. Examples: car on a banked curve, conical pendulum, satellite in orbit. For a banked road with no friction: tan(θ) = v²/(rg).
competences: Solve circular motion problems with banking
Explain why centripetal force is not a separate force

subtopics.name: Work-Energy Theorem and Conservation
subtopics.content: Work done: W = Fs cos(θ). Kinetic energy: KE = ½mv². Gravitational potential energy: PE = mgh. Elastic potential energy: PE = ½kx². Conservation of energy: total energy in an isolated system remains constant. Work-energy theorem: net work = change in KE. Power: P = W/t = Fv. Efficiency = (useful output / total input) × 100%.
competences: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces
Apply conservation of energy to solve problems

subtopics.name: Conservation of Momentum
subtopics.content: Linear momentum: p = mv. Conservation of momentum: total momentum before collision = total momentum after (in isolated system). Elastic collision: both momentum and KE conserved. Inelastic collision: momentum conserved, KE not conserved. Perfectly inelastic: objects stick together. Coefficient of restitution: e = relative speed of separation / relative speed of approach. e = 1 (elastic), 0 < e < 1 (inelastic), e = 0 (perfectly inelastic).
competences: Apply conservation of momentum to collisions

subtopics.name: Newton's Law of Gravitation
subtopics.content: F = GMm/r², where G = 6.674 × 10⁻¹¹ N m² kg⁻². Gravitational field strength: g = GM/r². Gravitational potential: V = -GM/r. Orbital velocity: v = √(GM/r). Escape velocity: v = √(2GM/r). Kepler's Third Law: T² ∝ r³, or T² = (4π²/GM)r³. Geostationary orbit: T = 24 hours, r ≈ 42,000 km from Earth's centre.
competences: Calculate orbital velocity and escape velocity

subtopics.name: SHM Equations and Energy
subtopics.content: SHM: acceleration proportional to displacement and directed toward equilibrium. a = -ω²x. Displacement: x = A sin(ωt + φ). Velocity: v = Aω cos(ωt + φ). Maximum velocity: v_max = Aω. Maximum acceleration: a_max = Aω². Period of mass-spring: T = 2π√(m/k). Period of simple pendulum: T = 2π√(l/g). Energy: E_total = ½kA² = ½mω²A². KE = ½mω²(A² - x²). PE = ½mω²x².
competences: Calculate period of springs and pendulums

subtopics.name: Wave Parameters and Types
subtopics.content: Wavelength (λ): distance between successive crests. Frequency (f): oscillations per second (Hz). Period (T = 1/f). Amplitude (A): maximum displacement. Wave speed: v = fλ. Transverse waves: displacement perpendicular to propagation (light, water). Longitudinal waves: displacement parallel to propagation (sound). Intensity ∝ A². Inverse square law: I ∝ 1/r².

subtopics.name: Interference and Diffraction
subtopics.content: Superposition: when waves overlap, resultant displacement = sum of individual displacements. Constructive interference: path difference = nλ. Destructive interference: path difference = (n + ½)λ. Young's double slit: fringe spacing Δy = λD/d. Single slit diffraction: minima at sin(θ) = nλ/a. Diffraction grating: d sin(θ) = nλ, where d = slit spacing.

subtopics.name: Stationary Waves
subtopics.content: Stationary waves form when two waves of same frequency travel in opposite directions. Nodes: points of zero displacement. Antinodes: points of maximum displacement. String fixed at both ends: L = nλ/2, f_n = nv/(2L). Open pipe: L = nλ/2. Closed pipe: L = (2n-1)λ/4 (odd harmonics only).
competences: Calculate resonant frequencies of strings and pipes

subtopics.name: Speed of Sound and Doppler Effect
subtopics.content: Sound is a longitudinal wave. Speed of sound in air ≈ 340 m/s at 20°C. Speed increases with temperature: v = 331 + 0.6T (°C). Doppler effect: observed frequency changes when source or observer moves. f' = f(v ± v_o)/(v ∓ v_s). Approaching: frequency increases. Receding: frequency decreases. Beat frequency = |f₁ - f₂|.
competences: Calculate speed of sound at different temperatures

subtopics.name: Reflection and Refraction
subtopics.content: Law of reflection: angle of incidence = angle of reflection. Snell's law: n₁ sin(θ₁) = n₂ sin(θ₂). Refractive index: n = c/v. Total internal reflection: sin(c) = n₂/n₁ (where c = critical angle). Applications: optical fibres, mirages, prisms. Thin lens formula: 1/f = 1/v - 1/u. Magnification: m = v/u. Power of lens: P = 1/f (in dioptres).
competences: Calculate critical angle for total internal reflection

subtopics.name: Coulomb's Law and Electric Field Strength
subtopics.content: Coulomb's law: F = kQ₁Q₂/r², where k = 1/(4πε₀) = 8.99 × 10⁹ N m² C⁻². Electric field strength: E = F/q = kQ/r². Electric potential: V = kQ/r. Potential difference: V = W/q. Uniform field between parallel plates: E = V/d. Field lines point from positive to negative. Equipotential surfaces are perpendicular to field lines.
competences: Calculate electric field strength and potential

subtopics.name: Ohm's Law and Resistance
subtopics.content: Ohm's Law: V = IR. Resistance: R = ρl/A (ρ = resistivity, l = length, A = cross-sectional area). Series: R_total = R₁ + R₂ + R₃. Parallel: 1/R_total = 1/R₁ + 1/R₂ + 1/R₃. Power: P = IV = I²R = V²/R. Kirchhoff's first law: ΣI = 0 at a junction. Kirchhoff's second law: ΣV = 0 around a loop. EMF = terminal PD + lost volts: ε = V + Ir.
competences: Apply Ohm's Law and Kirchhoff's laws

subtopics.name: Capacitors
subtopics.content: Capacitance: C = Q/V. Parallel plate: C = ε₀εᵣA/d. Energy stored: E = ½CV² = ½QV = ½Q²/C. Capacitors in parallel: C_total = C₁ + C₂. In series: 1/C_total = 1/C₁ + 1/C₂. Charging: Q = Q₀(1 - e^(-t/RC)). Discharging: Q = Q₀e^(-t/RC). Time constant: τ = RC.
competences: Solve series and parallel capacitor networks

subtopics.name: Faraday's and Lenz's Laws
subtopics.content: Magnetic flux: Φ = BA cos(θ). Faraday's law: EMF = -dΦ/dt = -N(dΦ/dt) for N turns. Lenz's law: induced current opposes the change that causes it. EMF in a rotating coil: ε = NBAω sin(ωt). Peak EMF: ε₀ = NBAω. Transformers: V₁/V₂ = N₁/N₂ = I₂/I₁ (ideal). Eddy currents: induced currents in bulk conductors, cause heating.
competences: Calculate induced EMF in rotating coils

subtopics.name: AC Circuits and RMS Values
subtopics.content: AC voltage: V = V₀ sin(ωt). RMS values: V_rms = V₀/√2, I_rms = I₀/√2. Average power: P = V_rms × I_rms × cos(φ). Reactance: capacitive X_C = 1/(ωC), inductive X_L = ωL. Impedance: Z = √(R² + (X_L - X_C)²). Resonance when X_L = X_C: f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)). Power factor: cos(φ) = R/Z.
competences: Solve AC circuit problems with impedance

subtopics.name: Photoelectric Effect
subtopics.content: Einstein's equation: E = hf = φ + ½mv²_max. Work function φ = hf₀ (threshold frequency). Planck's constant: h = 6.63 × 10⁻³⁴ J s. Stopping potential: eV_s = ½mv²_max = hf - φ. Photon energy: E = hf = hc/λ. Wave-particle duality: particles exhibit wave properties (de Broglie wavelength: λ = h/mv). Energy levels: E = -13.6/n² eV for hydrogen.
competences: Calculate stopping potential and threshold frequency

subtopics.name: Radioactivity and Nuclear Reactions
subtopics.content: Alpha (α): ⁴₂He nucleus, stopped by paper, highly ionising. Beta (β⁻): electron, stopped by aluminium. Gamma (γ): EM radiation, stopped by thick lead. Half-life: t½ = ln2/λ. N = N₀e^(-λt). Activity: A = λN = A₀e^(-λt). Mass-energy equivalence: E = mc². Binding energy per nucleon peaks at iron-56. Nuclear fission: heavy nucleus splits. Nuclear fusion: light nuclei combine. Both release energy.
competences: Describe three types of radioactive decay

subtopics.name: Gas Laws and Thermodynamics
subtopics.content: Ideal gas law: pV = nRT = NkT. Boyle's law: pV = constant (constant T). Charles's law: V/T = constant (constant p). Pressure law: p/T = constant (constant V). Kinetic theory: pV = ⅓Nm<c²>. Average KE = 3kT/2. First law of thermodynamics: ΔU = Q - W. Specific heat capacity: Q = mcΔT. Latent heat: Q = mL.
competences: Apply ideal gas law and gas laws
